CONTENT.Career: Initially a holding midfielder, Crama began his career in his native France in the Béziers youth system at U11 level and following a growth spurt, he was developed into a central defender. He progressed to the reserve team and made 6 Championnat National 3 appearances between October and December 2020, before being promoted into the first team squad and making five Championnat National appearances during the second half of the truncated 2019–20 season. Despite signing his first professional contract with the club in March 2020, Crama departed the Stade Raoul-Barrière in August 2020.On 25 August 2020, Crama moved to England to join the B team at Championship club Brentford on a two-year contract, with the option of a further year, for an undisclosed fee. Following two seasons of exclusively B team football, the option on Crama's contract was taken up in May 2022. On 30 June 2022, he signed a new two-year contract, with the option of a further year. Crama was including the first team squad for its 2022–23 pre-season and mid-season training camps and was an unused substitute during five regular season matches. A proposed loan to Ligue 1 club Montpellier during the winter transfer window was rejected.Following an appearance in the first team's opening 2023–24 pre-season friendly, Crama signed a new three-year contract, with the option of a further year. On 25 July 2023, Crama and Brentford teammate Matthew Cox joined League One club Bristol Rovers on season-long loans. Deployed predominantly as a starter when fit, Crama made 34 appearances during a mid-table 2023-24 season.Following two 2024–25 first team pre-season appearances and inclusion in the first team's squad's Portugal training camp, Crama joined League One club Exeter City on a season-long loan.
CONTENT.Honours: Brentford BLondon Senior Cup: 2021–22Premier League Cup: 2022–23
CONTENT.Personal life: Crama's father was a semi-professional footballer and coach.
CONTENT.External links: Tristan Crama at SoccerbaseTristan Crama at Brentford FCTristan Crama at Premier LeagueTristan Crama at foot-national.com

SUMMARY:
Tristan Crama (born 8 November 2001) is a French professional footballer who plays as a central defender for EFL League One club Exeter City, on loan from Premier League club Brentford.
Crama is a product of the Béziers academy and transferred to Brentford in 2020.
